refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task type, and environmental conditions to establish traceability.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide earthmoving capability (bucket capacity, cycle time, reach, force) sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor the Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to achieve productivity_target equivalent to on-site operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofence, load limits, emergency stop) that do not degrade productivity below productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry (position, load, cycle status, operator mode) to enable Site Manager to verify productivity KPI achievement during evaluation period.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize Vehicle hydraulic system (pump displacement, valve response, actuator sizing) to minimize cycle time and maximize bucket fill efficiency, paired with operator training and task scheduling to achieve target throughput.

DP-B: Implement integrated Teleoperation with low-latency video/control feedback and predictive motion assistance to enable remote operator to match on-site productivity, combined with Vehicle capability tuning.

DP-C: Design Safety Services as a constraint-enforcement layer (speed governors, load limiters, geofence) that operates transparently to operator commands, ensuring safety compliance without requiring operator intervention.

DP-D: Establish a closed-loop productivity monitoring system (Telemetry collection, real-time KPI calculation, dashboard reporting) that provides Site Manager visibility into productivity achievement and enables adaptive task scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B + DP-C + DP-D (integrated approach): Achieve productivity_target by combining (1) Teleoperation system with low-latency, high-fidelity operator interface to enable remote operation at parity with on-site productivity, (2) Safety Services as a transparent constraint layer that enforces worksite policies without operator burden, and (3) real-time Telemetry-driven productivity monitoring to enable Site Manager oversight and adaptive scheduling. Vehicle capability (DP-A) is a prerequisite but is treated as a given baseline; the decomposition focuses on the system-level integration that unlocks productivity.

potential_sources: Teleoperation latency and Safety Services constraint enforcement: if safety governors introduce unacceptable latency or restrict operator control range, remote operator productivity will degrade. Mitigation: Safety Services must operate asynchronously (predictive enforcement) rather than reactive blocking.

Telemetry bandwidth and Teleoperation control loop: high-frequency telemetry (video, sensor streams) may compete with low-latency control commands on Network Link. Mitigation: prioritize control channel; use adaptive telemetry sampling.

Productivity measurement accuracy and operator mode switching: if Telemetry does not clearly distinguish on-site vs. remote operation, productivity comparison will be confounded. Mitigation: explicit operator_mode flag in Telemetry.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP is a system-level integration pattern rather than a single design choice. Coupling is managed through clear interface contracts (Telemetry schema, Tasking command protocol, SafetyAlert trigger rules) and asynchronous constraint enforcement.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Vehicle baseline capability (hydraulic system, actuators, bucket) is sufficient to achieve productivity_target under ideal conditions (on-site operation, no safety constraints). If Vehicle is undersized, no system-level integration will recover productivity.
ASSUMPTION: Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency (<200 ms round-trip) to support low-latency Teleoperation. If network is degraded, remote operator productivity will degrade.
ASSUMPTION: Operator training and task scheduling are optimized independently. System design assumes competent operator and well-planned task sequence.

RISK: Safety Services constraint enforcement may introduce latency or restrict operator control authority, degrading productivity. Mitigation: design Safety Services as predictive (geofence, load limit) rather than reactive (emergency stop only).

RISK: Telemetry overhead (video streaming, sensor logging) may consume network bandwidth, reducing control responsiveness. Mitigation: implement adaptive telemetry sampling and prioritize control channel.
RISK: Tele-operator fatigue or skill gap may reduce productivity below on-site baseline. Mitigation: operator training program and fatigue monitoring via Telemetry.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Explicitly decompose DP-B into sub-DPs: (B1) low-latency control channel design, (B2) operator interface design (video, haptic feedback, predictive assistance), (B3) network protocol and error handling. This will enable clearer allocation to Teleoperation module and identification of critical performance requirements.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and supporting infrastructure (Network Link, Telemetry collection, Site Manager dashboard). It is responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery and compliance with worksite safety plan.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time stream of Vehicle state (position, load, cycle status, operator mode, productivity metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to Site Manager dashboard and external monitoring systems. Frequency: 1-10 Hz depending on metric. Includes operator_mode flag (on-site|remote) for productivity comparison.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: High-level task commands from Site Manager (e.g., 'excavate zone A', 'load truck B', 'move to staging area') that are decomposed by Teleoperation or on-site operator into low-level Vehicle control commands. Frequency: event-driven (task initiation, task completion).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Alerts and notifications from Safety Services to Site Manager and Tele-operator when safety constraints are triggered (geofence breach, load limit exceeded, emergency stop activated). Frequency: event-driven. Includes severity level and recommended action.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel between Vehicle (on-site) and Teleoperation (remote). Carries control commands (Tasking decomposition), telemetry streams, and safety alerts. Latency requirement: <200 ms round-trip for low-latency operation. Bandwidth: ~5-20 Mbps depending on video quality and sensor sampling.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary. Productivity KPI is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry. No diffusion; all sub-components are contained within NGHE Solution scope.

Vehicle provides the physical earthmoving capability (bucket, hydraulics, actuators) that is a necessary but insufficient condition for productivity_target. Vehicle alone cannot achieve productivity_target without Teleoperation (for remote operation) and Safety Services (for constraint enforcement). Productivity measurement requires Telemetry from Vehicle, but Vehicle does not own the measurement or reporting logic. Diffusion: productivity KPI is split across Vehicle (capability), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement).

Teleoperation enables remote operator to command Vehicle and monitor status via Telemetry. Teleoperation is critical for achieving productivity_target in remote operation mode, but does not own Vehicle capability or Safety Services enforcement. Productivity depends on Teleoperation latency, operator interface quality, and operator skill. Diffusion: productivity KPI is split across Teleoperation (operator interface), Vehicle (capability), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement).

Safety Services enforces operational constraints (geofence, load limits, speed governors) that must not degrade productivity below productivity_target. Safety Services is a constraint layer that operates transparently to operator commands. Productivity depends on Safety Services design (predictive vs. reactive enforcement). Diffusion: Safety Services is a cross-cutting concern that affects Vehicle control, Teleoperation responsiveness, and overall system throughput.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Productivity KPI (CR-NGHE-001) is a system-level acceptance criterion that cannot be fully contained within any single module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). The NGHE Solution boundary must own the integration logic, measurement, and verification of productivity_target achievement. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) own specific design parameters (capability, operator interface, constraint enforcement) that contribute to productivity, but the system-level KPI is owned by NGHE Solution.
containment_risks: RISK: Productivity measurement logic (Telemetry collection, KPI calculation, dashboard reporting) is not explicitly assigned to a module. If Telemetry is owned by Vehicle and dashboard is owned by Site Manager external system, the measurement chain is fragmented. MITIGATION: Assign Telemetry collection and KPI calculation to NGHE Solution or a dedicated Telemetry/Monitoring sub-module.

RISK: Productivity target is defined as a customer acceptance criterion, but the NGHE Solution does not own the customer's operational context (soil type, task profile, operator skill, weather). Productivity achievement depends on factors outside NGHE Solution control. MITIGATION: Establish clear assumptions about operational context in the evaluation protocol; define productivity_target as a conditional acceptance criterion (e.g., 'under representative conditions with trained operator').

RISK: Safety Services constraint enforcement may introduce latency or restrict operator control, degrading productivity. If Safety Services is not tightly integrated with Teleoperation and Vehicle control, the interaction may be unpredictable. MITIGATION: Design Safety Services as a predictive constraint layer (geofence, load limit) that operates asynchronously to operator commands, not as a reactive blocker.

RISK: Teleoperation latency and Network Link quality are critical to remote operator productivity, but Network Link is an external infrastructure dependency. If Network Link is degraded, productivity will degrade, but NGHE Solution may not have control over network provisioning. MITIGATION: Establish network performance requirements (latency, bandwidth, availability) as part of the deployment specification; design Teleoperation to degrade gracefully under network degradation (e.g., switch to on-site operation).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Decompose NGHE Solution into explicit sub-modules: (1) Vehicle (physical earthmoving capability), (2) Teleoperation (remote operator interface and control), (3) Safety Services (constraint enforcement), (4) Telemetry & Monitoring (measurement and reporting). Assign productivity measurement ownership to Telemetry & Monitoring module. Establish clear interface contracts between modules (Telemetry schema, Tasking protocol, SafetyAlert rules) to enable independent development and testing. Define productivity_target as a conditional acceptance criterion with explicit assumptions about operational context.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (productivity_target achievement with dual operator modes and safety compliance) is directly addressed by the selected DP (integrated Teleoperation + Safety Services + Telemetry monitoring). The DP provides a logical solution path: (1) Teleoperation enables remote operation at parity with on-site, (2) Safety Services enforces constraints transparently, (3) Telemetry enables measurement and Site Manager oversight. The DP is not over-constrained and does not introduce unnecessary complexity. Coupling risks are identified and mitigated through interface design (asynchronous Safety Services, prioritized control channel, explicit operator_mode flag).

diffusion_risks: Productivity KPI is diffused across Vehicle (capability), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). No single module owns the end-to-end productivity delivery. MITIGATION: NGHE Solution owns the integration and measurement; sub-modules own specific design parameters.

Telemetry collection and KPI calculation are not explicitly assigned to a module. If Telemetry is owned by Vehicle and KPI calculation is owned by Site Manager external system, the measurement chain is fragmented. MITIGATION: Assign Telemetry collection to Vehicle; assign KPI calculation to NGHE Solution or a dedicated Telemetry & Monitoring sub-module.

Safety Services constraint enforcement may introduce latency or restrict operator control, affecting Teleoperation responsiveness and productivity. If Safety Services is not tightly integrated with Teleoperation, the interaction may be unpredictable. MITIGATION: Design Safety Services as a predictive constraint layer that operates asynchronously to operator commands.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate system-level boundary for productivity KPI ownership. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to productivity but do not own the end-to-end KPI. Telemetry and Network Link are critical infrastructure that must be explicitly designed and tested. Ownership of Telemetry collection and KPI calculation should be clarified in the next decomposition level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Productivity_target achievement can be verified through representative operations trials: (1) conduct earthmoving tasks under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience), (2) measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) using Telemetry data, (3) compare against productivity_target. Verification requires clear definition of 'representative operations' and 'agreed evaluation period'. Verification is feasible if Telemetry provides accurate cycle time and load measurements.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Productivity KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution as a system-level acceptance criterion. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (capability), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement) are clear. Telemetry and Network Link are critical infrastructure with explicit performance requirements (latency <200 ms, bandwidth ~5-20 Mbps). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Decompose NGHE Solution into explicit sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry & Monitoring) with clear ownership and interface contracts.
Define productivity_target as a conditional acceptance criterion with explicit assumptions about operational context (soil type, task profile, operator skill, weather, network quality).
Establish network performance requirements (latency, bandwidth, availability) as part of the deployment specification.
Design Safety Services as a predictive constraint layer (geofence, load limit) that operates asynchronously to operator commands, not as a reactive blocker.
Assign Telemetry collection to Vehicle; assign KPI calculation and reporting to NGHE Solution or a dedicated Telemetry & Monitoring sub-module.
Establish operator training program and fatigue monitoring to ensure tele-operator productivity is not degraded by skill gap or fatigue.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing Vehicle capability, Teleoperation effectiveness, Safety Services constraint enforcement, and Telemetry measurement. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, but the IM assignment reveals that productivity KPI is diffused across multiple modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Further decomposition is necessary to allocate specific design parameters to each module and establish clear ownership of measurement and reporting. The NGHE Solution boundary is appropriate for system-level integration, but sub-modules must be explicitly defined with clear interface contracts.
decision.justification_detail: The gate review identified three key issues: (1) Productivity KPI is diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; no single module owns end-to-end delivery. (2) Telemetry collection and KPI calculation are not explicitly assigned to a module. (3) Safety Services constraint enforcement may introduce latency or restrict operator control, affecting Teleoperation responsiveness. These issues cannot be resolved at the current level; they require decomposition of NGHE Solution into sub-modules with explicit ownership and interface contracts. Decomposition will enable clearer allocation of design parameters and identification of critical performance requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide earthmoving capability (bucket capacity, cycle time, reach, force) sufficient to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor the Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to achieve productivity_target equivalent to on-site operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofence, load limits, emergency stop) that do not degrade productivity below productivity_target.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The Telemetry & Monitoring system shall collect real-time Vehicle state (position, load, cycle status, operator mode) and calculate productivity KPI (m³/h or ton/h) to enable Site Manager verification of productivity_target achievement.

assumptions: Vehicle baseline capability (hydraulic system, actuators, bucket) is sufficient to achieve productivity_target under ideal conditions (on-site operation, no safety constraints).
Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency (<200 ms round-trip) to support low-latency Teleoperation.
Operator training and task scheduling are optimized independently; system design assumes competent operator and well-planned task sequence.
Productivity_target is defined as a conditional acceptance criterion with explicit assumptions about operational context (soil type, task profile, operator skill, weather).
Safety Services constraint enforcement is designed as a predictive layer (geofence, load limit) that operates asynchronously to operator commands, not as a reactive blocker.
Telemetry collection and KPI calculation are owned by NGHE Solution or a dedicated Telemetry & Monitoring sub-module, not by external systems.
open_risks: Safety Services constraint enforcement may introduce latency or restrict operator control, degrading Teleoperation responsiveness and productivity.
Telemetry overhead (video streaming, sensor logging) may consume network bandwidth, reducing control responsiveness.
Tele-operator fatigue or skill gap may reduce productivity below on-site baseline.
Network Link degradation may reduce remote operator productivity; NGHE Solution may not have control over network provisioning.
Productivity measurement accuracy depends on Telemetry data quality; if Telemetry is inaccurate or incomplete, KPI calculation will be unreliable.
Productivity_target is a customer acceptance criterion that depends on factors outside NGHE Solution control (soil type, task profile, operator skill, weather).
evidence_needed: Network performance testing: latency, bandwidth, availability under representative conditions.
Teleoperation latency and operator interface testing: measure remote operator productivity vs. on-site baseline under controlled conditions.
Safety Services constraint enforcement testing: measure impact of geofence, load limits, speed governors on operator control authority and productivity.
Telemetry accuracy testing: validate cycle time, load, and position measurements against ground truth.
Representative operations trials: conduct earthmoving tasks under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience) and measure actual throughput.
Operator training program evaluation: assess tele-operator skill development and fatigue impact on productivity.
Network degradation testing: measure Teleoperation performance under degraded network conditions (latency, packet loss, bandwidth reduction).
